_comment: Circuit breaker defaults (spec Section 5.1). A breaker = (unit, window, max-events, trip-action). Every ceiling is a SAFETY CAP under Skill 60 Signal S4's law: a raise without an operator approval stamp is a P1; the system never silently raises its own limits. Overridable per box, but a loosened ceiling without a stamp is itself a P1.

process.watches: D1 restart velocity per supervised unit
process.note: STOP + park the unit (supervisor-native: max-restarts + exponential backoff restart delay) + P1 with last boot-log excerpt. A parked unit is visible-red until fixed; it never silently respawns. Feeds LP-B1/LP-B2 (LF-6).

turn.note: Heartbeat allowlist enforcement + offending cron parked + P1. NEVER touches the client's model choice (sovereignty) - it parks TIMERS, not models. Feeds LP-A2 (LF-8).

retry.note: Job parked in a resumable state + Rescue Rangers escalation (the auto-redispatch doctrine's own step 4: escalate after K tries, never tight-loop). Feeds LP-A4.

cron.note: Cron disabled (not deleted) + P1 with the cron id and its declared-vs-actual cadence. Feeds LP-A6/LP-C2/LP-D3 (LF-4).

healer.watches: the watchdog's OWN fix actions per target
healer.note: The session-health.sh lesson encoded: a healer that loops is disabled by its OWN breaker, never by discovering the damage later. > 3 fixes on the same target / 24h OR any fix-verify failure = STOP fixing that target, flag healer-loop-suspected, escalate.
